reasoning: The FRED series for US Commercial Real Estate Loans by All Commercial Banks (CREACBW027SBOG) has been extremely flat over the past year, hovering in the 2995–3011 range. Recent momentum since late February 2025 is slightly downward, dropping from ~3011 to roughly 3004 in early April. Given banks' continuing efforts to reduce CRE exposure amid higher rates and office market headwinds, short-term momentum favors a slight decrease or stagnation, supporting a <50% chance of an increase for the near-term resolution date (2025-04-20). The statistical model and seasonal trends generated by the FRED analysis yielded a combined P(increase) of 0.44 for the immediate period. In accordance with the forecasting rule that uncertainty should increase with the time horizon (pushing probabilities closer to 0.50 for distant dates due to the long-term random-walk nature of the series), the short-term estimates lean slightly bearish but smoothly approach a coin-flip at the 6-month horizon.
